action.content:
Name geometric shapes from their SVG paths.

Q: This SVG path element <path d="M 31.00,73.00 L 32.00,59.00 L 44.00,50.00 L 49.00,41.00 L 64.00,37.00 L 71.00,55.00 L 64.00,76.00 L 52.00,61.00 L 31.00,73.00"/> draws a
Options:
(A) circle
(B) heptagon
(C) hexagon
(D) kite
(E) line
(F) octagon
(G) pentagon
(H) rectangle
(I) sector
(J) triangle
A: (F)

Q: This SVG path element <path d="M 14.19,26.04 L 51.43,39.21 L 58.44,36.69 L 56.63,30.17 L 48.53,26.66 L 14.19,26.04"/> draws a
Options:
(A) circle
(B) heptagon
(C) hexagon
(D) kite
(E) line
(F) octagon
(G) pentagon
(H) rectangle
(I) sector
(J) triangle
A: (G)

Q: This SVG path element <path d="M 41.00,43.00 L 37.00,34.00 L 41.00,33.00 L 45.00,34.00 L 41.00,43.00"/> draws a
Options:
(A) circle
(B) heptagon
(C) hexagon
(D) kite
(E) line
(F) octagon
(G) pentagon
(H) rectangle
(I) sector
(J) triangle
A: (D)

Q: This SVG path element <path d="M 31.82,80.60 L 69.44,51.63 L 71.48,42.91 L 63.73,40.47 L 54.89,46.36 L 31.82,80.60"/> draws a
Options:
(A) circle
(B) heptagon
(C) hexagon
(D) kite
(E) line
(F) octagon
(G) pentagon
(H) rectangle
(I) sector
(J) triangle
A:
action.label: context

action.content:
To determine the shape, let's count the number of distinct vertices (points) defined in the SVG path command:

1.  `M 31.82,80.60` (Start/Vertex 1)
2.  `L 69.44,51.63` (Vertex 2)
3.  `L 71.48,42.91` (Vertex 3)
4.  `L 63.73,40.47` (Vertex 4)
5.  `L 54.89,46.36` (Vertex 5)
6.  `L 31.82,80.60` (Returns to Vertex 1, closing the shape)

The path connects 5 distinct points to form a closed polygon. A polygon with 5 sides and 5 vertices is a **pentagon**.

Comparing this to the options:
(A) circle
(B) heptagon (7 sides)
(C) hexagon (6 sides)
(D) kite (4 sides)
(E) line
(F) octagon (8 sides)
(G) pentagon (5 sides)
(H) rectangle (4 sides)
(I) sector
(J) triangle (3 sides)

The correct option is **(G)**.

A: (G)
action.label: response
